rationale: This page contains significant data on India's performance in two major global indices: the Global Gender Gap Index (implied by the dimensions) and the Global Hunger Index 2025. It includes specific statistics, rankings, and the methodology/composition of the GHI, making it highly relevant for polity and social issues questions.
cleanedText:
There is still a combined global average gender gap of over 30%.
- Overall, no economy has yet achieved full gender parity. It will take 123 years to reach full parity globally.
- In index, parity score of 1 indicates full parity and 0 denotes complete inequality.
### India’s Overall Performance (2025) by Key Dimension
| Key Dimension | India’s Overall Performance (2025) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Economic Participation & Opportunity | • Overall economic performance improved. • Economic participation rose marginally by 0.9% to 40.7% with higher parity in estimated earned income. • Labour Force Participation Rate remained unchanged at 45.9%, India’s highest so far. |
| Educational Attainment | • India scored 97.1%, indicating positive shifts in female literacy and tertiary education enrolment. |
| Health & Survival | • Higher parity achieved due to improvements in sex ratio at birth and healthy life expectancy. • Parity attained despite an overall decline in life expectancy for both men and women. |
| Political Empowerment | • Slight decline in parity (–0.6 points). • Women’s representation in Parliament fell from 14.7% to 13.8%. • Share of women ministers declined from 6.5% to 5.6%. |
## 8.11. GLOBAL HUNGER INDEX, 2025
### Why in the news?
Global Hunger Index (GHI) 2025 has been released by Concern Worldwide and Welthungerhilfe, Non-Government Organizations from Ireland and Germany respectively.
### COMPOSITION OF GHI SCORES AND SEVERITY DESIGNATIONS
- 1/3 Undernourishment + 1/6 Child stunting + 1/6 Child wasting + 1/3 Child mortality = GHI SCORE
### GHI Severity of Hunger Scale (100-point scale)
- ≤ 9.9: Low
- 10.0–19.9: Moderate
- 20.0–34.9: Serious
- 35.0–49.9: Alarming
- ≥ 50.0: Extremely alarming
### Key Findings of the report
#### India Related Findings:
- India is ranked 102nd among 123 countries assessed with a GHI score of 25.8, which is classified as serious.
- 1 in 3 Indian children is stunted. Undernourishment affects 172 million people, 13.5 million more than in 2016.
#### Global Findings:
- Achieving Zero Hunger: At current rates, SDG-2 (Zero Hunger by 2030) is out of reach. At least 56 countries are not on track to achieve even low hunger by 2030.
